research_question: After round 2 produced no new clean positive keeper under the frozen v18 operational contract, which additional atomic price-volume signals can expand the retained pool with truly complementary positive edge before any later family-construction round is reopened?

discovery_contract.builder_rule: each candidate builder may rank exactly one raw signal field and may not combine multiple raw signals inside the same candidate

signal_pool.interpretation: a shorter medium-term trend horizon may be more responsive than momentum_60_5_raw while still preserving clean continuation logic under the frozen v18 contract

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests whether the retained trend-strength mechanism becomes more actionable on a shorter continuation window.
dedup_note.override_reason: Registered as a shorter-horizon variant of the canonical medium-term momentum mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: a much longer trend horizon may produce thicker edge and more stable head separation than the currently retained momentum_60_5_raw signal

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests whether the same trend-strength mechanism thickens when stretched to a very long skip-month horizon.
dedup_note.override_reason: Registered as a long-horizon variant of the canonical momentum mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: a slower trend-consistency measure may behave more cleanly than the previously removed 20-day version and could complement trend strength without overreacting to short noise

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests whether the retired short-horizon trend-consistency mechanism becomes cleaner when slowed down materially.
dedup_note.override_reason: Registered as a slower-horizon variant of the earlier trend-consistency mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: a longer-horizon liquidity-improvement signal may preserve the positive edge found in liquidity_trend_20_60_raw while reducing head fragility and structural microstructure sensitivity

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests whether the retained liquidity-improvement mechanism becomes more stable on a longer window pair.
dedup_note.override_reason: Registered as a longer-horizon variant of the canonical liquidity-trend mechanism.

discovery_contract.aggregation_rule: none; this round explicitly forbids combining signals
discovery_contract.missing_data_rule: score nulls are allowed and must be preserved for audit; no imputation or cross-signal fallback is allowed

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard, same TopK=10 width, and same v18 top-15 refresh hysteresis
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same v18 refreshed-portfolio implementation until any later family round explicitly changes it

promotion_rule.phase_gate: Only atomic signals that show directionally coherent positive edge in signal-edge diagnostics may advance into a later family-construction round.
promotion_rule.non_promotion_rule: A signal that shows negative full-sample IC, inverted decile ordering, or clearly worse head labels than tail labels should be rejected rather than rescued by later extraction or refresh tweaks.

disallowed_changes: Do not mix multiple raw signals into one candidate inside this round.
Do not change the calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change the frozen v18 refresh hysteresis rule.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not add extraction, weight-penalty, turnover-suppression, or stability-cap changes inside this round.
Do not sweep multiple alternative directions for the same signal beyond the four preregistered hypotheses in this batch.
Do not introduce PIT quality, value, or investment fields into this round.
notes: This round starts only after round 2 completed with zero new clean positive keepers under the frozen v18 operational contract.
The retained atomic signal pool before round 3 still rests mainly on earlier discoveries such as momentum_60_5_raw and liquidity_trend_20_60_raw.
Round 3 therefore focuses on complementary horizon variants rather than repeating raw liquidity-level or low-volatility-level hypotheses that have already failed.
